weaknesses: Some literature_map entries (TREACLE, C3PO, compute_pareto2024, pareto_llm_errors2024) have placeholder author names -- citation-verifier should confirm real metadata
Multi-agent subsection relies on the claim that no topology-comparison studies exist, which is a negative claim difficult to fully verify
Pareto optimization subsection is slightly thinner than the other two -- could expand the contextual bandit connection to topology selection
DAAO and Dang et al. (2025) concurrent work distinction could be technically sharper
revision_priority: Verify placeholder citations (TREACLE, C3PO, etc.) have correct author/venue metadata
Sharpen DAAO distinction with specific technical contrast (operator-level vs topology-level)
Expand Pareto subsection with 1-2 more sentences connecting contextual bandits to the topology formulation
Add a sentence acknowledging Bayesian orchestration (bayesian_orchestration2026) if space permits

scores.quality_justification: All claims grounded in specific numbers from Results section (53% cost reduction, 95.6% retention, $58K/year savings, crossover at 0.35). Limitations are honest and specific -- six concrete items, not vague hedges. Future directions are actionable with named methods (contextual bandits, neural cost predictors). Deduction: the deployment cost estimate ($58K/year) assumes debate-level pricing for all 10K tasks which overstates savings if the baseline workload already uses cheaper topologies.
scores.clarity_justification: Four subsections with clear scope. Sentence length varies from 9 to 38 words. Active voice throughout. No filler phrases. Each limitation numbered for easy reviewer reference. Deduction: the crossover subsection is slightly dense and could benefit from one more paragraph break.
scores.significance_justification: Connects empirical findings back to theory (Simon's near-decomposability), positions against concurrent work (DAAO), and identifies a composable cost reduction strategy (FrugalGPT model axis + ParetOrch topology axis). The cross-model x cross-topology future direction is a genuine research contribution to the framing.

citation_stats.note: 5 citations at the lower end of the 5-8 target. Could add du2023debate when discussing topology costs or dang2025evolving in future directions, but current density is sufficient for a Discussion section where most content is interpretation rather than factual claims about prior work.

weaknesses.description: The $58K/year savings estimate assumes all 10K daily tasks would otherwise use debate topology. Real baselines may already use cheaper topologies for some tasks, reducing the marginal savings.
The claim that DIT crossover is model-agnostic is argued from first principles but not empirically verified on a second model family. A single data point (GPT-4o) supports a general claim.
Limitations list the 10-20% cost underestimate from billing overhead without citation or measurement. This number is plausible but ungrounded.
Future directions mention neural cost predictors and contextual bandits but do not discuss expected difficulty or feasibility of these extensions.

revision_priority: Qualify the $58K/year estimate with an explicit assumption statement about baseline topology
Soften the model-agnostic claim or add a caveat that verification on other model families is needed
Provide a source or measurement for the 10-20% billing overhead estimate in limitations
Consider adding 1-2 more citations (du2023debate, dang2025evolving) if reviewer flags low citation count

scores.quality_justification: All claims grounded in formal definitions or experimental data from pareto_results.json. Cost model derived from 756 runs with statistical validation (Kruskal-Wallis test). Mathematical notation consistent throughout. Algorithm pseudocode is complete and implementable. Deduction: multiplicative cost model is a simplification that reviewers may challenge.
scores.clarity_justification: Five subsections follow clean progression: formalize -> model cost -> model quality -> combine -> summarize. Every symbol defined on first use. Algorithm box provides self-contained summary. Sentence length varies well (8-30 words). Deduction: feature expansion phi(d) could use a worked example for accessibility.
scores.originality_justification: Constrained optimization framing (minimize cost subject to quality floor) is clean but not technically novel -- standard feasibility-then-optimize pattern. Originality lies in applying it to topology selection with DIT features as context. Logistic regression quality predictor is deliberately simple.

citation_stats.note: 2 citations used; within lower bound for methods section where most content is original formulation. Could add du2023debate or chen2023frugalgpt if reviewer flags low count.

weaknesses.description: Multiplicative cost model assumes constant overhead ratio across difficulty tiers. Real deployments may have non-multiplicative cost components (context window overflow, retries, rate limiting).
Logistic regression is argued to be sufficient but not empirically compared against stronger classifiers (XGBoost, small MLP) within this section.
DIT feature extraction treated as given input. Assumption that d(x) is available before routing is non-trivial; extraction cost stated as <5ms without measurement detail.
Pairwise interaction terms in phi(d) included without justification from feature selection or ablation. Contribution only implicitly covered by ablation in Section 5.

revision_priority: Add empirical comparison of logistic regression vs. stronger classifiers if reviewer requests
Discuss non-multiplicative cost factors (retries, context overflow) in Discussion/Limitations
Provide DIT extraction latency measurement or cite Paper 1 for extraction methodology
Cross-reference pairwise feature ablation when Section 5 is drafted
Consider adding 1-2 more citations (du2023debate for debate cost, chen2023frugalgpt for cascade framing) to hit 3-5 target
